events.title: Choose JSONL over SQLite for hot path
Adopt Gitleaks regex set for redaction
events.description: Covers AWS, GCP, GitHub PATs and OpenAI-style sk- keys.

events.title: Append race on hook reentry
events.description: Two PostToolUse fires within the same ms interleaved writes.

events.title: Add proper-lockfile around append
events.description: OS-level lock — survives reentry even within one process.

events.title: JSONL appends need OS-level locks even within one process

payload.promptSummary: Curate Gitleaks-derived regex set tuned to LogBook payload shape.
payload.fullPrompt: You are a redaction-rules curator. Given the Gitleaks core regex set, output a JSON list of patterns tuned for LogBook event bodies. Constraints: never strip natural English with the word 'token' in it; always preserve markdown code fence delimiters; cap each pattern at 200 chars to avoid catastrophic backtracking. Output JSON only.
payload.response: Selected 47 patterns; added 3 LogBook-specific rules (Notion API tokens, Sentry DSN, Linear API keys). All patterns capped at 180 chars. Verified zero false positives on the seeded transcript corpus.

chapters.label: W1 — Hook performance hardening
chapters.goal: Bring PostToolUse hook p95 under 200 ms
chapters.outcome: p95 from 312 ms → 87 ms after JSONL append batching

events.title: Catch + degrade silently with state.json warning
Hooks must NEVER exit non-zero — degrade silently
p95 is a contract, not a metric
Should we keep the legacy fallback append path?

payload.question: Should we keep the legacy fallback append path?

options.label: Yes — defensive
options.description: Keep the synchronous fs.appendFileSync fallback in case proper-lockfile fails to bind on exotic filesystems (NFS, FAT32).

options.label: No — dead code
options.description: Delete it. Two append paths double the surface area for race bugs.

events.title: System fonts only — no web fonts (ADR-7)
Reorder app.css imports — tokens BEFORE @tailwind base
Tailwind @layer order is load-bearing for CSS-var theming

payload.promptSummary: AUDIT 11 motion moments against prefers-reduced-motion + mobile graceful-degrade.
payload.fullPrompt: Run the design-motion-principles AUDIT in CREATE mode. Verify every motion moment has a reduced-motion fallback AND a mobile fallback. Output the 11-moment table with VERDICT column (pass | fail | needs-work). For any fail, propose a one-paragraph remediation.
payload.response: All 11 moments documented. 9 pass cleanly. 2 marked needs-work: (1) the chapter-header parallax needs an explicit transform: none in reduced-motion (currently relies on global @media kill). (2) DecisionMilestone pulse keeps the IntersectionObserver registered in reduced-motion — should bail out at observer creation. Both noted as P4 follow-ups.

events.title: Synthesize at READ path, not in a hook (ADR-5)
How should we cap notes length?

payload.question: How should we cap notes length?

options.label: 4 KB UTF-8
options.description: Matches existing redaction layer's body cap.

options.label: 8 KB UTF-8
options.description: Doubles space, doubles risk of leaked context bleed.

options.label: No cap
options.description: Free-text answers stored verbatim; redaction still runs.

payload.notes: Matches existing redaction layer's body cap; revisit if real transcripts hit it.

events.title: Cast to Record<string,unknown> at the boundary
Document type-cast escape hatches with the upstream issue ref

options.label: Yes — start with currentColor only
options.description: Light SVG only at first; dark added if smoke test flags contrast.

options.label: No — ship dual-SVG now
options.description: Pay the +50 % storage cost upfront; never revisit.

payload.promptSummary: Design the AskUserQuestion → agent_question pairing algorithm.
payload.fullPrompt: Design the algorithm that pairs `tool_use.name === "AskUserQuestion"` with its matching `tool_result` by tool_use_id, then splits an N-question call into N agent_question events. Constraint: must run in O(n) over the transcript JSONL; must redact secrets in the notes block; must truncate to 4KB. Output the algorithm as pseudocode plus the test cases needed.
payload.response: Single-pass scan over transcript JSONL with an askMap<tool_use_id, AskUseRecord>. On tool_use(AskUserQuestion) → askMap.set. On matching tool_result → parse answers block via regex `Your questions have been answered: "(.+?)" = "(.+?)"`, parse annotations block, emit N events. Redaction runs before persistence. Test cases: 1-question, 3-question, Other+notes, sk-* in notes, multi-byte UTF-8 truncation at 4KB boundary.

chapters.label: W3 — Editorial shell
chapters.goal: TOC + sidebar + 3-state sort + theme toggle + hash router

events.title: Svelte 5 runes can't live at module top-level — use $state in components

bodies.e1-001:
JSONL beats SQLite for this hot path because appends are line-buffered and there's no schema migration when we add a new event kind. The cost is that range queries become a linear scan, but our access pattern is sequential (chronological replay), not random.

Decision recorded for posterity:

  • Append-only — replayable from disk in O(n)
  • Zero schema migration — a new event kind is a JSON field
  • Redaction runs in the hot path, not in a downstream worker
bodies.e2-001: Each PostToolUse hook accumulates events in an in-memory buffer that flushes on the next tick. The trade-off: events may be lost if the process is SIGKILL'd between flushes. Acceptable because the transcript JSONL is the durable source of truth and events are derived from it on the next logbook build.
bodies.e3-003:
The bug: @tailwind base was imported BEFORE the token files, so Tailwind's reset rules used var(--color-text-primary) before the variable was declared. Older Safari reported the CSS-variable as unset and rendered body text in black on black.

The fix is two lines reordered. The lesson is that cascade order is load-bearing when you mix CSS variables with a utility framework.
